mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS path signals easier discounting and lower real-rate pressure on equity valuations, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is falling than when it is flat or rising, or if the sign flips in more than half of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which raises recession risk and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than negative returns, or if the conditional return differential is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates and HOUST remains firm, the data indicate resilient consumer spending and housing demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of improving RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling estimation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and inventory restocking, which supports earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day forward returns do not cluster after sustained INDPRO gains, or if the predictive slope is zero or negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration with FEDFUNDS still near the lower bound signals real-rate compression and delayed policy tightening, which supports equity multiples and positive short-horizon S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger does not precede positive 5-day returns more often than the unconditional base rate, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS rises above 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in PAYEMS growth combined with weak RSAFS growth marks a demand deceleration that reduces expected revenue growth and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns negative and RSAFS 3-month growth falls below its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if the combined weak-labor/weak-sales state fails to identify negative 20-day returns more frequently than the base rate, or if either proxy alone fully explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, rising UNRATE with falling PAYEMS signals recessionary stress, causing multiple compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if combined labor deterioration does not predict negative 60-day returns better than standalone labor series, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 50% in recession-like periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing rebound regime, rising INDPRO together with improving HOUST indicates broad cyclical recovery, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO/HOUST signal fails to deliver positive 60-day returns more often than the unconditional baseline, or if the interaction coefficient is not robust across rolling windows.
